title: Warden Sentry Benchmark: Runtime Efficiency & Recall
description: A visual summary of the Warden benchmarking article, emphasizing the Opus 4.8 high-effort Claude SDK vs Pi comparison.

best_recall.sql: SELECT model || ' (' || runtime || CASE WHEN effort <> 'default' THEN ', ' || effort ELSE '' END || ')' AS run, known_found, recall_pct FROM warden_benchmark ORDER BY known_found DESC LIMIT 1

lowest_cost_per_known.sql: SELECT model || ' (' || runtime || CASE WHEN effort <> 'default' THEN ', ' || effort ELSE '' END || ')' AS run, cost_per_known FROM warden_benchmark WHERE known_found > 0 ORDER BY cost_per_known ASC LIMIT 1

recall_by_run.sql: SELECT model || ' / ' || runtime || CASE WHEN effort <> 'default' THEN ' / ' || effort ELSE '' END AS run, known_found, recall_pct, recorded_cost, cost_per_known FROM warden_benchmark ORDER BY known_found DESC, recorded_cost ASC

cost_by_run.sql: SELECT model || ' / ' || runtime || CASE WHEN effort <> 'default' THEN ' / ' || effort ELSE '' END AS run, recorded_cost, known_found, cost_per_known FROM warden_benchmark ORDER BY recorded_cost ASC

duration_by_run.sql: SELECT model || ' / ' || runtime || CASE WHEN effort <> 'default' THEN ' / ' || effort ELSE '' END AS run, p50_s, p90_s, total_duration_m FROM warden_benchmark ORDER BY p50_s ASC

opus_costs.sql: SELECT runtime, value AS usd FROM opus48_high_runtime_comparison WHERE metric = 'Total recorded cost' ORDER BY value ASC

opus_context.sql: SELECT runtime, value AS k_tokens_per_turn FROM opus48_high_runtime_comparison WHERE metric = 'Input tokens per turn' ORDER BY value ASC

opus_recall.sql: SELECT runtime, value AS count FROM opus48_high_runtime_comparison WHERE metric IN ('Known vulnerabilities found', 'Final findings emitted') ORDER BY metric, runtime

opus_detail.sql: SELECT metric, runtime, value, unit, note FROM opus48_high_runtime_comparison ORDER BY metric, runtime

benchmark_table.sql: SELECT model, runtime, effort, known_found, recall_pct, total_findings, recorded_cost, cost_per_known, p50_s, total_duration_m FROM warden_benchmark ORDER BY known_found DESC, recorded_cost ASC

props.markdown:
## What the benchmark says
Warden scans 156 analysis chunks from 6 historical Sentry commits against an 86-item known vulnerability corpus. The strongest visual takeaway from the article is that **runtime context footprint matters**: in the Opus 4.8 high-effort pair, Pi found more known issues at far lower cost, even though it used more turns and tools.

## Opus 4.8 high: Claude SDK vs Pi
Both rows scanned the same 156 chunks with zero failures. Pi found **21/86** known vulnerabilities and emitted **24** findings. Claude SDK found **17/86** and emitted **17** findings. The SDK's extra cost came mostly from larger repeated context: about **60.0k** scan input tokens per turn vs **12.1k** for Pi.
